codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of admin event CRUD. 4 files added (1582 lines): 2 API route files, 1 page component, 1 test file with 18 tests. All required endpoints (GET/POST /api/admin/events, GET/PUT/DELETE /api/admin/events/[id]) implemented with proper auth gates (401/403), input validation, and type-specific field handling. UI includes event list table with all required columns, create/edit modal with conditional fields per event type, and delete confirmation dialog. Code follows existing patterns established by admin-products feature closely. Interactive verification confirmed events appear in correct user-facing tabs (football only in Football tab, etc.).

issues.description: Uses `any` type for `eventData` object. Could use `Prisma.EventCreateInput` for type safety. Same pattern exists in the admin products route, so this is consistent with project conventions.
Uses `any` type for `updateData` object. Could use `Prisma.EventUpdateInput` for type safety. Consistent with existing admin products pattern.

Price validation uses `!price` which treats 0 as falsy, but since price must be > 0, this is functionally correct. A more explicit check like `price === undefined || price === null` would be clearer but is not required.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Admin API routes duplicate auth checking logic (getServerSession + role check) in every handler function. Both admin/products and admin/events follow this pattern. This could be extracted into a shared `requireAdmin` helper, but since both features consistently use the same inline pattern, it's not a gap — just a potential improvement.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: app/api/admin/events/route.ts:7-24 vs app/api/admin/products/route.ts:7-24 — identical auth checking code. Same duplication in [id]/route.ts files for GET/PUT/DELETE handlers.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Both admin CRUD features (products and events) use `any` typed data objects when building Prisma create/update payloads. AGENTS.md says 'Use TypeScript strict mode throughout' but this pattern is now established across two features. If this is intentional for pragmatism, it should be noted; if not, it's a minor convention gap.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: app/api/admin/events/route.ts:113 — `const eventData: any = {`, app/api/admin/events/[id]/route.ts:137 — `const updateData: any = {}`. Same pattern in admin/products routes.

summary: PASS. The admin event management feature is well-implemented and fully covers the feature description requirements. All CRUD operations (create, read, update, delete) work for football, basketball, and concert event types with proper admin role enforcement. The UI provides a complete table view, create/edit modal with conditional type-specific fields, and delete confirmation dialog. 18 comprehensive tests cover auth gates, validation, and type-specific creation. The code follows established project patterns from admin-products. Only non-blocking issues found: use of `any` types in API routes (consistent with existing codebase pattern) and a minor clarity issue with price validation. No blocking issues.
